Event: Marvin considers killing Butcher because he sees him as a dangerous loose cannon
Explanation: Protects himself/team from perceived threat but escalates internal danger and mistrust.

Event: Butcher brutally kills Neuman and steals the Supe Virus
Explanation: Gains no clear benefit while unleashing a genocidal tool and destabilizing politics.

Event: Butcher decides to get back into the game after Hughie reveals Neuman is a supe and admits Butcher was right
Explanation: Rejoining furthers his vengeance goals but drags others back into lethal conflict.

Event: Butcher pushes Ryan away out of fear he’ll drag the boy down with him
Explanation: He sacrifices his bond and Ryan’s stability but still fails to protect him long term.

Event: Butcher goes sober after Becca’s death
Explanation: Improves his own functioning and reliability for others.

Event: Butcher launches a violent crusade against supes based on the mistaken belief Becca was murdered
Explanation: Harms himself and many others on a false premise with no lasting gain.

Event: Butcher devotes Season 2 to rescuing Becca
Explanation: Emotional focus for him and a chance for Becca, but ultimately costs time and leaves both worse off.

Event: Butcher spends most of Season 3 trying to weaponize Soldier Boy against Homelander
Explanation: He risks his life and team for a plan that collapses and causes collateral deaths.

Event: Butcher abandons using Soldier Boy when Ryan is endangered
Explanation: Sacrifices his best shot at Homelander to spare Ryan, helping Ryan but losing his main goal.

Event: Butcher overuses Temp V despite risks and shortens his life to 12–18 months
Explanation: Gains temporary power at the cost of terminal illness while only marginally aiding others.

Event: Butcher leads the team with an iron fist, prioritizing his goals and expecting obedience
Explanation: He advances his agenda at the cost of trust, morale, and team safety.

Event: Butcher leaves the others to Vought’s mercy to go after Homelander in Season 1
Explanation: He increases his own chance at vengeance by abandoning his team to severe risk.

Event: Butcher tries to sell Kimiko’s brother out in Season 2
Explanation: He seeks leverage for his cause while betraying allies and endangering an exploited target.

Event: Butcher tells Kimiko she is “exactly” a gun and demands total obedience
Explanation: Reinforces his control at the cost of their autonomy and relationship.

Event: Butcher leaves Joe Kessler for dead in the Panjshir Valley
Explanation: Saves himself/mission at the cost of a comrade’s life and later psychological damage.

Event: Butcher cheats on Becca with a waitress
Explanation: Momentary gratification undermines a central relationship and his own self-respect.

Event: Butcher beats Gunpowder to death in a parking lot
Explanation: Eliminates a dangerous supe but deepens his own spiral and public/official danger.

Event: Butcher threatens Homelander by vowing to hurt his loved ones to his face
Explanation: Small reputational gain and intimidation at the price of escalating a stronger enemy.

Event: Butcher kills Translucent through a brutal plan with the Boys
Explanation: Removes a lethal supe but greatly increases blowback on himself and teammates.

Event: Butcher viciously chews out a support group for giving supes leeway
Explanation: He vents and reinforces his worldview while retraumatizing victims and alienating allies.

Event: Butcher becomes a Big Bad and takes the Shetty Virus to wipe out all supes
Explanation: He dooms others and likely himself with a world-ending plan offering no stable benefit.

Event: Butcher forms a deal with Soldier Boy to kill Homelander despite MM’s trauma
Explanation: Pursues his vendetta by weaponizing MM’s abuser, worsening MM’s mental harm.

Event: Butcher saves Homelander from Soldier Boy after Ryan is hurt
Explanation: Protects Ryan but directly spares his arch-enemy, undercutting his whole mission.

Event: Butcher admits Ryan accidentally killing Mallory breaks him and he gives in to Kessler
Explanation: He abandons his better impulses and embraces a self-destructive genocidal path.

Event: Butcher leaves Ryan with Mallory to break the cycle of bad parenting
Explanation: He sacrifices his own desire for Ryan to secure a safer, healthier upbringing for the boy.

Event: Butcher lies to M.M. that he hasn’t contacted Frenchie to manipulate him into joining
Explanation: He gets MM back on the team but corrodes trust through obvious deceit.

Event: Butcher becomes a Boomerang Bigot by taking Temp V then killing Gunpowder for being a supe
Explanation: He embraces the power he hates while murdering another supe, intensifying his hypocrisy and risk.

Event: Butcher tells Ryan he hates him “to keep him safe”
Explanation: His cruelty backfires, pushing Ryan toward Homelander and undermining Becca’s wishes.

Event: Butcher knocks Hughie out to prevent him overdosing on Compound V
Explanation: He harms their immediate mission flexibility to protect Hughie’s life.

Event: Butcher recruits Frenchie again specifically to modify the virus so it won’t go airborne
Explanation: He consciously adds safeguards, limiting genocide risk while still targeting key threats.

Event: Butcher kills Stillwell by detonating explosives despite the baby being nearby (attempted murder)
Explanation: He chooses a suicidal, collateral-heavy tactic that neither kills Homelander nor secures gains.

Event: Butcher convinces Hughie to bug Vought by framing it as a red-pill choice
Explanation: He propels Hughie into effective but extremely dangerous espionage.

Event: Butcher refuses to hand over evidence to the CIA because they won’t prosecute Homelander
Explanation: His all-or-nothing stance delays justice and enables Vought’s cover-up.

Event: Butcher later cooperates with the CIA after supes-terrorists appear, when it’s too late
Explanation: Partial cooperation comes after the strategic window closes, offering limited benefit.

Event: Butcher reverts to killing/violence in Season 3 after Homelander provokes him
Explanation: He abandons a safer path and reignites a losing war that shortens his life.

Event: Butcher tries to dissuade the Sage Grove patient and only shoots after Hughie is nearly killed
Explanation: He initially de-escalates, then uses lethal force only when Hughie’s life is in danger.

Event: Butcher appears to prepare to shoot a man to steal his car but lets Starlight do the killing instead
Explanation: He enables a practical but lethal robbery, outsourcing the moral/psychic cost.

Event: Butcher uses a superpowered baby as a weapon to kill Vought guards
Explanation: He gains a tactical escape at the price of extreme collateral violence and ethical erosion.

Event: Butcher keeps taking Temp V to “level the playing field”
Explanation: Gains short-term parity while inflicting irreversible brain damage on himself.

Event: Butcher drugs M.M. so Marvin can’t interfere with the Soldier Boy plan
Explanation: He secures his strategy by violating MM’s autonomy and safety.

Event: Butcher offers Crimson Countess to Soldier Boy knowing she’ll be killed
Explanation: He trades her life as currency to maintain his alliance and goal.

Event: Butcher spares Termite and allows him to be arrested instead of executing him
Explanation: He restrains his usual brutality, supporting legal consequences over murder.

Event: Butcher frees the Temp-V–experimented rabbit from its cage
Explanation: He relieves the animal’s immediate suffering, albeit without foreseeing the mutation.

Event: Butcher later stomps the mutated rabbit to end its suffering
Explanation: He gains nothing but quickly ends a horrific death for the animal.

Event: Butcher gives Ryan Becca’s St. Christopher medallion as a keepsake
Explanation: He sacrifices his last memento to comfort Ryan and honor Becca’s memory.

Event: Butcher lets Kenji go to save Hughie
Explanation: He prioritizes Hughie’s survival while giving the captive supe a chance to escape.

Event: Butcher decides not to hand Ryan over to Vought in the Season 2 finale
Explanation: He abandons a self-serving deal to protect Ryan from exploitation.

Event: Butcher protects Ryan from Homelander with only a crowbar
Explanation: He risks his life for Ryan with no realistic chance of success.

Event: Butcher attempts a suicide-attack on Homelander with Stillwell as bait
Explanation: He seeks death while hoping to hurt Homelander, but the plan fails and harms bystanders.

Event: Butcher tries to commit suicide-by-Black Noir after Becca chooses Ryan
Explanation: He seeks death, which would leave his team more vulnerable, until Hughie’s presence stops him.

Event: Butcher changes his mind on suicide when he realizes the Boys will die with him
Explanation: He abandons self-destruction to prevent his friends’ deaths.

Event: Butcher knocks Zoe aside while killing Neuman in front of her
Explanation: He traumatizes and injures a child while gaining only short-term tactical advantage.

Event: Butcher threatens Vogelbaum’s entire family to force testimony
Explanation: He increases odds of cooperation but by horrifically endangering innocents.

Event: Butcher smashes Becca’s headstone despite her family’s pleas
Explanation: He vents his own pain by destroying their mourning place, hurting everyone emotionally.

Event: Butcher confronts a Christian at Believe Expo and attacks their faith verbally
Explanation: He satisfies his misotheistic anger while distressing an uninvolved stranger.

Event: Butcher threatens to kill Laser Baby later if it “doesn’t keep its nose clean” (verbal)
Explanation: Dark joke/implicit threat reinforces his violent persona with no real benefit.

Event: Butcher sells drugs as a teen and beats the headmaster with a stapler when caught
Explanation: He resists authority and protects his pride at the cost of severe consequences.

Event: Butcher leaves Lenny behind to join the military, believing it will reduce their father’s abuse
Explanation: He seeks escape but miscalculates, worsening Lenny’s fate and his own guilt.

Event: Butcher moves from trying to cure his cancer to embracing Kessler and the tumor’s power
Explanation: He trades possible acceptance of death for becoming host to a genocidal force.

Event: Butcher rips Victoria Neuman apart in front of Zoe
Explanation: He removes one threat but traumatizes a child and helps enable Homelander’s coup.

Event: Butcher threatens Frenchie to obtain the virus after killing Neuman
Explanation: He secures the weapon by coercing an ally and alienating the last people who might stop him.

Event: Butcher kicks his entire team aside at tentacle-point when they plan to work with Neuman
Explanation: He destroys his last support network while gaining only short-term control.

Event: Butcher tries one last time to appeal to Ryan to oppose Homelander
Explanation: His attempt is sincere and pro-social but fails and contributes to his despair.

Event: Butcher accepts he will include himself among the supes to die if he releases the virus
Explanation: He is willing to die for his genocidal plan, harming everyone including himself.

Event: Butcher risks exposing himself in Season 5 Episode 1 to save Hughie from Homelander
Explanation: He jeopardizes his own covert goals to prevent Hughie’s immediate death.
